--- SOURCE 1: Professional Development for Public Sector Employees & Staff ---
URL: https://metrostateipd.org/professional-development-for-public-sector-employees/

SUMMARY:
Public Sector Design-Thinking Training. This customized learning experience not only teaches human-centered designed concepts, but uses your own service

FULL CONTENT:
![Customized Employee Training for St. Paul and Minneapolis Businesses](https://metrostateipd.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/IPD-rectangle-color-logo-160.png)
![Customized Employee Training for St. Paul and Minneapolis Businesses](https://metrostateipd.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/IPD-white-logo-for-header.png)

# Professional Development for Public Sector Employees & Staff

Research is clear that recruiting and keeping public sector employees is getting more and more difficult.  See our article series on the [*Public Sector Recruiting Challenge*.](https://metrostateipd.org/public-sector-news-and-trends/)

Once you recruit good people, the next challenge is to retain them.  Providing professional development for your public sector employees, not only helps with retention, it can help build a pipeline of future leaders.

Below are some options for professional development, but if you do not see what you need, please contact IPD so that we can build a training experience that both you and your employees will value.

![](https://metrostateipd.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/pub-sec-5-300x293.png "pub sec 5")

## Public Sector Supervision Certificate

#### Complete all 12 courses for an E-badge and convert your badge to graduate credit at Metro State University.

A professional development package built with the Association of Minnesota Counties and the League of Minnesota Cities to specifically address the needs of public sector employees.

## Public Sector Project Management Training

A learning solution built with experiential learning.  Immerse future project managers into the fictional City of Willard and learn to navigate project management with the complexities that face public sector employees.

## Public Sector Continuous Improvement Training

Employee development incorporates Plan, Do, Study, Act strategy with a “Learn and Do” workshop style training.

## Public Sector DEI Training

#### Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion are embedded in public sector work.

From official policies and laws to hearing citizen voices to treating fellow employees with respect.  Start with our DEI Workplace Essentials Certificate or have us customize training to meet your needs with our instructors who appreciate the unique qualities of public sector work.

## Public Sector Facilitation Training

#### For other organizations, facilitation is a “game-changing leadership skill;” for the public sector it is a required skill for almost every role since you serve the public needs.

Facilitation skill enhancement takes your meetings from endless conversation to dynamic sessions that gather input from all stakeholders and lead to action steps for managing change, strategic initiatives, or projects.

## Public Sector Design-Thinking Training

This customized learning experience not only teaches human-centered designed concepts, but uses your own service audience to apply the skills to your work.  Leave with work that can be used to improve your customer experience.  Taught by instructors who understand how public sector differs from for-profit corporations.

#### Request More Information

![](https://metrostateipd.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/IPD-white-logo-for-header-300x78.png)

## Institute for Professional Development

# Design thinking training

**Empowering Innovation in the Public Sector** Unlocking Creative Solutions for Complex Challenges Through Design Thinking

##### WHAT'SÂ NEW?

##### DESIGN THINKING WEBINAR

###### Why design thinking?

Design thinking empowers public sector organizations to tackle complex challenges with human-centered solutions.   
  
By fostering creativity, collaboration, and user empathy, it helps government agencies innovate, improve services, and create more impactful policies that meet the real needs of citizens.   
  
This approach drives efficiency, enhances problem-solving, and fosters a culture of continuous improvement in public service delivery.

--- SOURCE 3: Design Thinking for Public Service Innovation ---
URL: https://www.globalgovernmentforum.com/design-thinking-for-public-service-innovation/

SUMMARY:
This course is designed to help you to understand how you can use design thinking in your role and as part of your team to deliver even better public services.

FULL CONTENT:
# Design Thinking for Public Service Innovation

![](https://www.globalgovernmentforum.com/wp-content/uploads/DT6-1024x682.jpg)

***This is an open programme and an in-house training course, which we can deliver either online or in person. For further information and pricing contact [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) or phone on +44 20 7661 7817.***

![](https://www.globalgovernmentforum.com/wp-content/uploads/CCS_2935_Supplier_AW_72dpi-1.jpg)

Global Government Forum, as part of Pendragon International Media Ltd, have been named as a supplier on Crown Commercial Service’s Learning & Training Services DPS Framework. All our Learning and Training services are included in the framework.   
Crown Commercial Service supports the public sector to achieve maximum commercial value when procuring common goods and services.

**Overview**

One of the most important approaches to emerge in public policy development in recent years is known as design thinking. Design thinking is particularly focused on how we can shape and organise public services and public administration to better serve the needs of users and citizens through innovation and collaboration.   
  
While many approaches to public service performance focus on how to secure incremental improvement, design thinking seeks to drive radical transformation, drawing on the insight and experience of service users and communities to fundamentally reshape delivery models at a time when governments and the public sector are facing unprecedented fiscal and demographic challenges. The course will show that approaches based on design principles are appropriate for governments across the world.

**Who is the seminar designed for?**

The seminar is designed for public servants working at all levels in policy roles, who want to bring their thinking and approaches right up-to-date with the most modern and innovative approaches to policy formulation, design, evaluation, and delivery. The principles of best practice in policy making are evolving in today’s public services, and this course is designed to help you to understand how you can use design thinking in your role and as part of your team to deliver even better public services.

**How will you benefit from attending?**

The seminar will be useful to both policy team members and leaders. You will understand the main principles of design thinking, and how to approach these and apply them in the context of the civil service and wider public sector. You will benefit from understanding the different phases involved, preparing you for implementing these methodologies in your role. You will learn from the analysis of case studies, highlighting design thinking in practice, and understand how to prepare for, and overcome, some of the challenges you may face.

**Learning Outcomes**

You will:

**Agenda**

**Session One: General Principles of Design Thinking**  
Elaboration of the main principles of design thinking  
How these can be applied to the management of public services and the role of government  
How do governments tend to approach design thinking?  
What are the benefits, and how do we realise these most effectively?  
How do we unleash the potential of innovation by using design thinking approaches?  
Questions and discussion  
 **Session Two: The Different Phases of the Design Thinking Process**

Outline of the main phases of the design thinking process, and how to apply it in civil service and wider public sector roles  
Examination of some different approaches that have commonly been used in public sector contexts, along with their pros and cons, their implications, and their transferability  
Questions and discussion

**Session Three: Case Studies on How to Apply Design Thinking in the Public Sector**Presentation and analysis of a range of case-studies where design thinking has been applied in practice in public services in a variety of national and organisational contexts  
The role of practical tools and methods  
Fostering a culture of innovation in public service design and delivery  
Questions and discussion

**Session Four: Overcoming Challenges and Barriers**Reflections on the challenges that practitioners might face in adopting design thinking principles in their everyday work  
A look at the main barriers and obstacles for public sector teams and leaders in seeking to re-shape public services  
Final questions and discussion

**Close**

**Our L&D ethos**

**Our L&D Team**

**Our L&D seminars**

# Design Thinking Certificate for the Public Sector

**Based on the Spring2 Design Thinking Methodology, this certificate program will give you the tools needed to apply design thinking in the public sector effectively.**

Learn from innovation experts how to modernize programs, policies, and services, while increasing resilience and adapting to change – from a human-centered perspective.

[Register](#upcoming-sessions)[Download Course Brochure](https://spring2innovation.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/Spring2-Design-Thinking-Certificate-Brochure.pdf)

The Spring2 Design Thinking Certification will give you the tools to understand end clients better, see your business through the lens of your customers, and provide greater value – giving you the tools you need to stay three steps ahead of your competition.

Throughout this certificate program, participants will be expertly led through each phase of the design thinking process and develop a deep understanding of the tools and methods applied to working with customers and internally. Various case studies and activities will ensure a deep understanding among the participants.

A workbook will be used to track learning and guide participants in applying concepts to a real challenge they face at work. To support this, coaching sessions will be provided, offering personalized guidance to refine strategies and develop effective solutions.

Completion of all modules, workbook exercises, and submission of applied knowledge will be required for certification. Upon evaluation, participants will be awarded the Spring2 Design Thinking Certification.

#### Design Thinking 101

#### Getting to the Root of the Problem

#### Define the Problem & Generate Solutions

#### Prototyping Real-World Solutions

#### Test, Test, Test!

This certificate is for government individuals and teams who want to learn how to understand and solve complex challenges, through a user-focused, and empathy driven lens. Past participants include:

enriched_points.expanded_content: Public sector employees require tailored training in design thinking to foster innovation, enhance service delivery, and effectively address complex challenges faced by communities.
Many public sector workers struggle with creative problem-solving, limiting their ability to generate innovative solutions that effectively meet the diverse needs of citizens.
Ethnographic research, crucial for understanding user needs, is often underutilized, hindering the public sector's capacity to design services that are genuinely user-centered and impactful.
